description: Sector overlay for payment service providers, electronic money, clearing/settlement participants, fintech payment platforms, merchant payment products, and payment operations in Togo.

metadata.scope: Togo-only sector overlay controls for Payment Platforms and Services. Intended to be used with togo_core_control_library.
metadata.source_note: Source basis is mapped from Togolese and applicable regional legal/regulatory sources available at rule-pack publication time. This starter pack is not legal advice and requires expert validation before production reliance.
metadata.external_knowledge_policy: No external knowledge is introduced during document evaluation. Regulatory source mappings live only in this versioned rule pack.

primary_sources.name: BCEAO Regulation No. 15/2002/CM/UEMOA on payment systems in WAEMU member states
BCEAO payment systems surveillance policy materials

limitations: Rules encode document-evidence checks, not a final legal determination.
Each rule requires validation against current Togolese laws, regional WAEMU/OHADA/CIMA instruments, regulator circulars, directives, codes and guidance before production reliance.
Automated results are screening signals only and require qualified human legal/compliance review.

rules.title: Payment-service or electronic-money authorization evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for BCEAO authorization, EME status, payment-service approval, licence, or regulated status evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified Togolese legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, current-law status, and legal sufficiency before relying on the automated result.

rules.title: Payment system, clearing, or settlement controls are documented
rules.summary: Checks for payment-system participation, clearing, settlement, SICA-UEMOA, STAR-UEMOA, reconciliation, or settlement risk evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified Togolese legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, current-law status, and legal sufficiency before relying on the automated result.
source_basis: BCEAO Regulation No. 15/2002/CM/UEMOA on payment systems in WAEMU member states

rules.title: Customer identification, KYC, or transaction-monitoring evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for KYC, customer due diligence, suspicious transactions, sanctions screening, or transaction monitoring.

rules.title: Payment security, fraud, or incident-management evidence is documented
rules.summary: Checks for payment security, fraud management, incident response, authentication, access control, or operational resilience evidence.

rules.title: Customer disclosure, fees, complaints, or dispute handling evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for terms, fees, limits, customer support, complaints, reversals, chargebacks, or dispute-resolution evidence.
